ail: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimele.
aim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimol.
all: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Allar.
alms: noun. Something given to the poor as charity, such as money, clothing or food.
als: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tosk Albanian.
ami: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Amis.
amiss: adjective. Wrong; faulty; out of order; improper or otherwise incorrect.
ash: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Abishira.
ass: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ipulo.
hail: noun. Ball s or pieces of ice falling as precipitation, often in connection with a thunderstorm.
ham: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hewa.
has: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Haisla.
hill: noun. An elevated landmass smaller than a mountain.
him: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Himachali languages.
hiss: noun. A sibilant sound, such as that made by a snake or escaping steam; an unvoiced fricative.
ill: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranun.
ism: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masimasi.
lam: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lamba.
las: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gur Lama.
lash: noun. The thong or braided cord of a whip, with which the blow is given.
lass: noun. A girl; also, a young woman.
lima: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Lima of the ICAO / NATO radiotelephony alphabet.
lis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lisu.
mails: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of mail
mall: noun. A pedestrianised street, especially a shopping precinct.
mash: noun. A mass of mixed ingredients reduced to a soft pulpy state by beating or pressure; a mass of anything in a soft pulpy state.
mil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Peñoles Mixtec.
mill: noun. A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc. (Some are small and simple, and some are large and complex.)
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
miss: verb. To fail to hit, catch, grasp, etc.
missal: noun. A prayer book; a church service (book).
sail: noun. A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along.
sal: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Salishan languages.
salmi: noun. A rich stew or ragout, especially of game.
sash: noun. A piece of cloth designed to be worn around the waist.
sha: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Shall-Zwall.
shall: verb. Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.
shill: noun. A person paid to endorse a product while pretending to be impartial.
sial: noun. The rocks rich in silicon and aluminum that form the upper layer of the earth's crust, which lies beneath all continental landmasses.
sill: noun. A breast wall; window breast; horizontal brink which forms the base of a window.
sim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (New Guinea).
sima: noun. The upturned edge of a roof which acts as a gutter; a cyma.
sis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siuslaw.
sisal: noun. A Central American plant, Agave sisalana, cultivated for its sword-shaped leaves that yield fibers used for rope.
slam: verb. To shut with sudden force so as to produce a shock and noise.
slash: noun. A slashing action or motion: A swift, broad cutting stroke, especially one made with an edged weapon or whip.
slims: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of slim
small: adjective. Not large or big; insignificant; few in number.
smash: noun. The sound of a violent impact; a violent striking together.
